coin_profile.name: Internet Computer
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Internet Computer (ICP) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #57 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Margin of safety present — price near or below speculative fair value during accumulation (deep bear) phase.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 5.3/10 and survivability 63.3%. Base: 7.0% p.a., Bull: 13.0% p.a., Bear: -21.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.3/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-21.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 63.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

watchlist_signal.reason: Quality Cash-Flow Protocol with a margin of safety present — price near speculative fair value during a constructive cycle phase.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 7.5/10 and survivability 73.3%. Base: 10.0% p.a., Bull: 17.0% p.a., Bear: -15.8% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (10.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (7.5/10). Bull (17.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 73.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Fee-based methods (Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue) used DeFiLlama protocol fee data to anchor the valuation. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: InfiniFi USD (IUSD) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #295 by market cap.

dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Cycle phase (Insufficient Data) not favourable for entry — wait for accumulation or early uptrend.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.5/10 and survivability 53.3%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -29.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.5/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-29.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 53.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) did not contribute to the median: the implied fair value fell outside the 0.1x–10x price bounds (volume is very low or very high relative to circulating supply). Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 1 method contributed to the median — the valuation should be treated as a rough anchor, not a precise fair value. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Historical price data unavailable for this token. Forecast chart shows projected values only.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: MEET48 carries a Dangerous rating because its fundamentals score only 4.5/10 as a Cash-Flow Protocol, with notable weaknesses in protocol maturity. Significant future token dilution potential adds further pressure. Dangerous-rated tokens carry a higher risk of permanent capital loss and should only be held with strict position sizing and a clear exit plan.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: MEET48 is a Cash-Flow Protocol with weak fundamentals, scoring 4.5/10 overall. Its clearest strength is network demand, while protocol maturity remains the main area to watch. Key risks: future token dilution (FDV/MC ratio: 2.7x); low protocol maturity score (1/10); only 37% of supply currently circulating. MEET48 warrants caution and should only be held as a small position with a clear thesis and defined risk limits.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: MEET48 scores 4.5/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.
